description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, tan gable roof with two prominent stone chimneys and a mix of stone and timber framing on the exterior walls. The facade is adorned with multiple rectangular windows, each framed with wooden shutters, and the stonework is composed of large, rounded stones set in a regular pattern. The front of the house includes a covered porch with timber supports and a small gabled dormer above the entryway. The side elevations reveal additional overhangs and small canopies above secondary doors or windows, adding architectural interest. The structure appears to have three stories, with the uppermost level partially integrated into the roof space. The overall design suggests a blend of rustic and medieval European influences, with a focus on sturdy materials and decorative timber elements.
description: A three-story stone and timber house with a steep gable roof, two chimneys, and a covered front porch, featuring multiple shuttered windows and rustic architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, symmetrical window arrangement, and both chimneys rising from the roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep roof, a small dormer window, and a secondary canopy over a side entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying additional windows, a side canopy, and the full height of the structure with visible timber framing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the main porch, dormer, and the stonework pattern on the facade.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with regular spacing and wooden shutters
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with timber supports, a gabled dormer above, and a single main door framed by stone and wood
